description: Golden NL-to-SQL cases scored against evals/fixture.db. Ground truth = result of expected_sql, compared order-insensitively.

cases.question: Show the names of all customers who live in Karachi.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE city = 'Karachi'

cases.question: How many orders are there in total?
How many orders were cancelled?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'cancelled'

cases.question: How many customers are there in each city? Show the city and the count.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) FROM customers GROUP BY city

cases.question: List the names of customers who have at least one completed order, without duplicates.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed'

cases.question: How many orders were placed in May 2024?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', order_date) = '2024-05'

cases.question: What is the total revenue from completed orders? Revenue is quantity times unit price.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(oi.quantity * p.price) FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed'

cases.question: Show the total revenue for each product category from completed orders. Return the category and the revenue.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * p.price) FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category

cases.question: Which customer or customers placed the most completed orders? Return the name and the number of completed orders.
cases.expected_sql: WITH counts AS (SELECT c.name AS name, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name) SELECT name, n FROM counts WHERE n = (SELECT MAX(n) FROM counts)

cases.question: What is the average product price per category? Return the category and average price.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT category, AVG(price) FROM products GROUP BY category

cases.question: Which product or products have been ordered in the largest total quantity across all orders? Return the product name and the total quantity.
cases.expected_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT p.product_name AS product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS q FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name) SELECT product_name, q FROM totals WHERE q = (SELECT MAX(q) FROM totals)

cases.question: Which cities have more than 2 customers? Return the city and the customer count.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) FROM customers GROUP BY city HAVING COUNT(*) > 2

cases.question: List the names of customers who have never placed any order.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM orders)

cases.question: For order 6, list the product names and quantities it contains.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.product_name, oi.quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id WHERE oi.order_id = 6

cases.question: How many customers signed up in March 2024?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', signup_date) = '2024-03'
